includes: runtime and collaboration surfaces that the final site implies

notes: This milestone freezes the 45-state initial scrape baseline.
From this point forward, work should treat scraping as a completed baseline and move into data governance, truth maintenance, and blocker preservation.

executiveSummary.publicDataHeadline: The repo has strong national program, routing, nonprofit, advocate, and condition foundations, but provider coverage, normalization, knowledge depth, and workflow/runtime layers are still thin or empty in the checked-in DB.
criticalGaps: Providers and care are still the biggest final-website data hole.
Competitive-help families are not live in the checked-in DB at all.
Knowledge content remains too thin for a fully guided national family journey.
Directory foundation metadata is modeled well but sparsely populated, especially on nonprofits and advocates.
Normalization is present but too shallow to power a truly location-rich final website.
Advocates remain truth-sensitive and still block California from strict gold.
Runtime/feedback/product-support tables are mostly empty, so signed-in workflows are not final-product complete.

publicRouteSurfaces.needs: Trust framing, search entry points, strong downstream data for benefits, county help, and knowledge journeys.
publicRouteSurfaces.mainGap: Not data-blocked itself; value depends on downstream public surfaces staying truthful and deep.

publicRouteSurfaces.needs: Providers, nonprofits, advocates, directory-foundation metadata, truthful local contact paths, and competitive-help categories.
publicRouteSurfaces.mainGap: Providers are thin, competitive-help is not live, and nonprofit/advocate metadata is sparse.

publicRouteSurfaces.needs: Programs, eligibility, documents, steps, appeals, forms, waitlists, waiver typing, DD routing, and knowledge support.
publicRouteSurfaces.mainGap: Waitlists are shallower than the rest of the program layer and waiver typing is still sparse.

publicRouteSurfaces.needs: County offices, DD routing, education routing, local nonprofits, truthful providers, and public-truth gating.
publicRouteSurfaces.mainGap: Routing is strong, but county-level local care/provider depth remains thin.

publicRouteSurfaces.needs: Truth-safe advocate records, county coverage, contact signals, specialties, and public-eligibility gating.
publicRouteSurfaces.mainGap: Advocate count is broad, but truth-safe local depth is still an active blocker and California remains strict-gold blocked here.

publicRouteSurfaces.needs: Condition taxonomy, functional-needs mapping, linked programs, routing, and knowledge content.
publicRouteSurfaces.mainGap: Reference taxonomy is strong, but knowledge depth and competitive-help depth are still limited.

publicRouteSurfaces.needs: Official forms, official library roots, appeal/download URLs, and state coverage accounting.
publicRouteSurfaces.mainGap: Live form count is strong, but only 7 states are fully cleared and 37 are still fallback-authoring states.

publicRouteSurfaces.needs: Appeal info, deadline logic, waitlists, official forms, and knowledge explainers.
publicRouteSurfaces.mainGap: Appeal rows are strong, but waitlist depth and knowledge explainers still need more depth.

publicRouteSurfaces.needs: Knowledge content, program/routing context, and runtime persistence where applicable.
publicRouteSurfaces.mainGap: These surfaces exist, but supporting content/runtime depth is still uneven.

publicRouteSurfaces.needs: Family cases, child profiles, reminders, document tracking, collaboration threads, and sharing tokens.
publicRouteSurfaces.mainGap: Schema exists, but most runtime tables are empty or demo-only in the checked-in DB.

familyAudits.label: Truth and public eligibility contract
needs: Every public page must be source-backed and non-synthetic.
Indexing, sitemap inclusion, and render eligibility must follow the same truth rule.
Verification metadata must remain complete for public-serving layers.
currentHave: 5/50 BLOCKED states held in noindex launch posture
45 index-safe states in the current launch contract
staging: Partial-gated blocked states: arizona, alaska, maine, idaho, new-hampshire
queue: No direct scrape lane; enforced through truth audits, render gating, sitemap policy, and promotion rules.
familyAudits.gap: The remaining launch work is not broader indexing. It is keeping the 5 blocked states visible only through explicit partial-state gating without implying local proof.

familyAudits.label: Geography and coverage foundation
needs: 50-state coverage, full county coverage, and junction-based service-area mapping without fake county cloning.
queue: No major geography scrape backlog remains in the broad runnable universe.
familyAudits.gap: Foundation-grade. Keep using junction tables instead of fake local duplication.

familyAudits.label: Programs, benefits, appeals, and forms
needs: Program definitions, eligibility rules, document requirements, application steps, appeals, forms, and public-safe program pages.
currentHave: Program verification mix: verified=170; source_listed=63; official_verified=25
queue: Launch forms accounting: 7 cleared, 6 ready exact, 37 author-first fallback
familyAudits.gap: Programs and forms are structurally strong, but forms exact-state coverage is not finished and generic agency-page overpromotion still has to stay suppressed.

familyAudits.label: Waivers and waitlists
needs: Explicit waiver identity, source-backed action paths, waitlist or interest-list visibility, and official linkage where applicable.
currentHave: 0 ready rows unresolved; 8 typed live waiver rows
Waitlist launch exact states: florida; georgia; ohio; pennsylvania; illinois; texas
queue: Waiver closure: 16% state coverage / 100% queue closure
Waitlist closure: 100% identified / 100% followup queue closure
familyAudits.gap: Waitlists are materially shallower than the broader program layer, and waiver typing/state-level waiver resolution is still incomplete.

familyAudits.label: County offices, DD routing, and education routing
needs: Truthful county office lookup, statewide DD/IDD intake paths, regional or district education routing, and complete trust metadata.
currentHave: Office verification mix: generated_county_fallback=651; needs_review=49; source_listed=14; official_verified=6
State routing verification mix: source_listed=57; verified=33; official_verified=30
staging: 50/50 state coverage live; 0 ready rows unresolved
47/50 regional state coverage; 0 ready rows unresolved
queue: 50/50 state coverage live; 0 ready rows remain; 40 repair rows remain
familyAudits.gap: This is one of the strongest public layers, but regional education is still 47/50 states and final repair-ledger cleanup remains.

familyAudits.label: Providers and care
needs: Truthful anchor providers per state, named clinics/programs, contact and location evidence, and eventually deeper care discovery.
currentHave: Launch provider standard: 0 launch-ready states; 10 pull-now planned; 42 authored targets; 1 discovery-only row
familyAudits.gap: This remains the clearest data hole for the final website. Provider rows exist in all states, but county depth and launch-ready anchor coverage are still far from finished.

familyAudits.label: Nonprofits and parent-support organizations
needs: Broad local support coverage, trustworthy contact paths, and richer intake/accessibility metadata.
currentHave: Nonprofit verification mix: source_listed=1390; needs_review=343; generated_county_fallback=42
familyAudits.gap: Coverage breadth is strong, but nonprofit metadata remains sparse and there is no separate live competitive-help table yet.

familyAudits.label: Advocates and legal / IEP support
needs: Truth-safe advocate records, specialties, counties served, contact details, and public-safe legal/IEP support presentation.
familyAudits.gap: Advocate volume is high, but truth-safe local advocate depth is still explicitly blocked and directly affects final website confidence.

familyAudits.label: Housing, goods, jobs, care, and other competitive-help families
needs: Live website rows for housing, goods/supplies, jobs/vocational, care/independent living, and transport/utilities/food.
Actionable service/contact evidence, not just taxonomy or topic tags.
currentHave: Completion dispositions: housing=explicitly_blocked, goods=explicitly_blocked, jobs=explicitly_blocked, care=explicitly_blocked, transport=queued
queue: Runnable universe leftovers: housing=0, goods=0, jobs=0, care=0, transport=0
familyAudits.gap: This is a major final-website gap: the site model wants these families, but the checked-in live DB has no dedicated public help-resource table and no staged rows.

familyAudits.label: Directory foundation metadata
needs: Service taxonomy, audience tags, availability, next-step instructions, accessibility, capacity, claim state, and trust freshness across listings.
currentHave: Provider accessibility signals: accepts_medi_cal=0/0; interpreter_available=0/0; asl_available=0/0; wheelchair_accessible=0/0; virtual_services=0/0; in_person_services=0/0; home_visits=0/0; transportation_help=0/0
Nonprofit accessibility signals: interpreter_available=0/0; asl_available=0/0; wheelchair_accessible=0/0; virtual_services=0/0; in_person_services=0/0; home_visits=0/0; transportation_help=0/0

Advocate accessibility signals: interpreter_available=0/137; asl_available=0/137; wheelchair_accessible=0/137; virtual_services=0/137; in_person_services=0/137; home_visits=0/137; transportation_help=0/137
familyAudits.gap: Schema support is excellent, but live nonprofit and advocate accessibility/intake/capacity signals are still near-zero in the checked-in DB.

familyAudits.label: Org -> program -> location normalization
needs: Canonical organizations, org-program links, service locations, office locations, and virtual service areas that support location-rich discovery without false local claims.
familyAudits.gap: Normalization exists, but service-location depth is still far too thin for a fully location-rich final website.

familyAudits.label: Conditions, needs, and matching support
needs: Condition taxonomy, functional-need taxonomy, age/insurance references, and rule-backed links from disabilities or needs to programs/routing.
currentHave: reference tables present; no direct queue
queue: Dependency-verification family: 100% direct data present / 0% dependency verification complete
familyAudits.gap: The reference layer is strong; the remaining gap is downstream depth in programs, waivers, providers, and knowledge.

familyAudits.label: Knowledge content and guidance
needs: Structured explainers for diagnosis, waivers, school rights, appeals, respite, transition, and related family journeys, with provenance-safe serving.
currentHave: 2 promoted live; 54 blocked; 3 unresolved; provenance not yet launch-safe
familyAudits.gap: Live article volume is still small, and launch planning still flags provenance-safe topic coverage as unresolved.

familyAudits.label: Sources, review, and operational feedback loops
needs: Healthy source registry, source verifications, user submissions, coverage gaps, and verification queues to sustain the final website.
familyAudits.gap: Operational maintenance loops are mostly modeled, but almost empty in live data.

familyAudits.label: Family cases, child profiles, reminders, and document tracking
needs: Real runtime support for cases, children, tracking, documents, reminders, and waiver vault flows if the final website includes signed-in family workflows.
familyAudits.gap: This is mostly demo-level runtime data today, not a fully live family workflow layer.

familyAudits.label: Support planning, collaboration, and shared portal flows
needs: Collaboration threads, share tokens, clinical documents, IEP accommodations/goals, and respite/planning tools if the final website includes family coordination.
familyAudits.gap: These collaboration/planning surfaces are essentially schema-only in the checked-in DB.

notableRuns.summary: Queue burned down from 486 to 6; 469 accepted and 467 staged.
Queue burned down from 129 to 0; 41 accepted and 41 staged.
JS-heavy tail burned from 3 to 1; two rows became blocked and one remains.

immediateTruths: 50/50 modeled completeness does not mean all information categories are deeply built out.
0/50 strict-gold states means 45 states still sit in the public-safe-but-blocked lane on the stricter truth registry.
The named launch milestone `national-initial-scrape-v1` freezes the current launch baseline at 45 COMPLETE states, 5 BLOCKED states, and 45 index-safe states.
Provider coverage and knowledge-content depth are still the largest visible product information gaps.
Directory metadata exists in schema, but most nonprofit and advocate rows still lack rich accessibility and capacity signals.
Several workflow and support layers are still mostly empty despite having schema support.
